Stub functions for Bluetooth lock operations, including lock, unlock, and status checks.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that declares and defines a set of functions related to a lock mechanism, likely for thread synchronization. The functions [`bt_lock`](<#bt_lock>) and [`bt_unlock`](<#bt_unlock>) are placeholders for locking and unlocking operations, but they currently do not perform any actions. The function [`bt_lock_assert_held`](<#bt_lock_assert_held>) takes a boolean parameter `is_held` and is intended to assert whether the lock is held, though it does not implement any logic. The function [`bt_lock_is_held`](<#bt_lock_is_held>) returns a boolean value indicating the lock status, but it is hardcoded to return `false`. This file is intended to provide an interface for lock management, but the actual lock functionality is not implemented.
